questionHtml: Calculate the standard Gibbs free energy change, ΔGθ, in kJ mol−1, for the first dissociation of citric acid at 298 K, using section 1 of the data booklet.
markschemeHtml: «ΔGθ = −RT ln K = −8.31 J K–1 mol–1 × 298 K × ln(5.01 × 10–4) ÷ 1000 =» 18.8 «kJ mol–1» ✔

meta.title: Calculate the standard Gibbs free energy change, ΔGθ, in kJ mol−1, for the first dissociation of citric acid at 298 K, using section 1 of the data booklet.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.3—How far? The extent of chemical change
Reactivity 2.3.7—The equilibrium constant and Gibbs energy change, ΔG, can both be used to measure the position of an equilibrium reaction.
